region_of_origin: United States (McDonald's global chain; Singapore market variant)
food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal with sides (burger, potato wedges/fries, beverage)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger entry (FOODON_00002383). No specific entry exists for a creamy mushroom double-patty burger meal with sides. Closest parent match used; meal components (fries, beverage) are not individually coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers hamburgers and similar products. No specific code exists for a creamy mushroom double-patty burger meal set. Closest parent match used at the burger-product level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for a fast-food mushroom double burger meal. SNOMED CT is not used here to avoid code fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One full meal set consisting of a Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe burger (approximately 280 g), a serving of Star Potatoes/potato wedges (approximately 120 g), and a medium soft drink (approximately 250 g/ml), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fast Foods, Hamburger; double, large patty; with condiments and vegetables (FDC ID 21318) combined with USDA FoodData Central — Fast food potato wedges (FDC ID 21138) and standard soft drink values; weighted composite estimate for meal set
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger Meal. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite: (1) USDA FDC 21318 — Fast Foods, Hamburger, double large patty with condiments and vegetables, used as the burger component proxy; (2) USDA FDC 21138 — Fast food potato wedges, used as the Star Potatoes proxy; (3) USDA FDC 14400 — Carbonated beverage, cola, used as the soft drink proxy. Per-100g values are derived from the weighted composite of the three components at approximate serving weights of 280 g burger, 120 g potato wedges, and 250 g soft drink (total 650 g). Confidence is medium due to estimation from analogous USDA entries.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; white wheat bread and mixed fast-food burger meal analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger Meal. GI is estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate sources: white wheat burger bun (GI ~70–75 per University of Sydney GI Database for white bread analogues) and potato wedges (GI ~60–65 for fried potato products per University of Sydney GI Database). The soft drink component (cola, GI ~63) also contributes. A blended weighted GI of approximately 65 is applied for the composite meal. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 143 g carbohydrate in 650 g meal) / 100 ≈ 93. This is a high glycemic load meal. Estimation basis documented per Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~2470 mg per meal set)
hypertension.guidance: The full meal set provides approximately 2470 mg of sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg in a single sitting; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal and opt for lower-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load (~93 GL units per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 93 per meal set and approximately 143 g of refined carbohydrates from the bun, potato wedges, and soft drink, this meal is likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike and is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without careful portion control and substitution of the soft drink with water.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and total caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The meal set contains approximately 20.8 g of saturated fat and ~2 g of trans fat per serving alongside high sodium, making it a high cardiovascular risk meal; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose grilled, lower-fat alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger Meal is a fast-food meal set sold at McDonald's Singapore outlets, available via field survey delivery. The meal consists of a double beef patty burger topped with a rich creamy mushroom sauce, sautéed mushrooms, lettuce, cheese, and mayonnaise, served in a soft wheat bun, accompanied by Star Potatoes (seasoned potato wedges) and a medium soft drink. The burger patties are grilled beef, and the mushroom cream sauce gives the burger a savoury, umami-rich flavour profile with a creamy, indulgent texture. The potato wedges are crispy on the outside and fluffy inside, seasoned with herbs and spices. The meal is certified Halal and is a popular indulgent fast-food option in Singapore. Nutritionally, the full meal set is calorie-dense (approximately 1268 kcal per set), high in sodium (~2470 mg), high in saturated fat (~20.8 g), and delivers a very high glycemic load (~93 GL units), making it a high-concern meal for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is ultra-processed and contains allergens including gluten, milk, egg, and soy. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on low-sodium or low-glycaemic diets. Per 100 g, the composite meal provides approximately 195 kcal, 8.5 g protein, 8.8 g fat, and 22 g carbohydrate.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger Meal as a composite set. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of three analogous USDA FDC entries (double large patty hamburger with condiments, fast food potato wedges, and cola soft drink) at approximate serving weights. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white bread and fried potato products. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. This record covers the full meal set (burger + Star Potatoes + soft drink) as sold by McDonald's Singapore via field survey. Per-100g values represent the weighted composite of all three components at approximate serving weights of 280 g (burger), 120 g (potato wedges), and 250 g (soft drink), totalling 650 g. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this composite estimate. 5. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed', as this is a composite fast-food meal set and not a vegetable dish. 6. GI is estimated at 65 (medium) from analogous University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread and fried potato products; glycemic load per serving is approximately 93 (high), calculated as (65 × 143 g carbohydrate per 650 g meal) / 100. 7. The meal is Halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. 8. Allergens include gluten (wheat bun), milk (cream sauce, cheese), egg (mayonnaise), and possible soy in patty seasoning. 9. Price from field survey data: SGD 15.75 (fixed price across both listed outlets). 10. Ontology codes are closest parent matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for this specific meal set.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected food_group from 'Vegetables' (input error) to 'Mixed' as this is a composite fast-food meal set.
Iteration 1: Added mandatory Singaporean multilingual local_names: chinese_simplified, chinese_traditional, malay, tamil, hindi.
Iteration 1: Built weighted composite nutrient estimate from three USDA FDC entries (FDC 21318 double hamburger, FDC 21138 potato wedges, FDC 14400 cola) at serving weights 280g/120g/250g.
Iteration 1: Assigned GI 65 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread and fried potato products; calculated GL per serving as 93.
Iteration 1: Added FoodOn FOODON_00002383 and FoodEx2 A0FHR as approximate ontology anchors with gap notes; set SNOMED CT to null with honest gap note.
Iteration 1: Populated all three health_context conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) with specific clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes field.
Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all strings in aliases_by_language confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases list. No discrepancies found.
Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to comply with G5 hard gate validation rules.
